titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 728

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=968 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:12:46.055464+00 | translation moa=969 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:12:46.046075+00

p.cl: Omnibus fidelibus Willelmo de ueteri ponte salutem .

Sciatis quod penitentia ductus reddo deo et sancto
cuthberto et monachis eius de coldingham terram illam de qua contentio fuerat inter me
et eosdem monachos tempore dauid Regis . quam ego iniuste super eos occupauream . ab omni
calumpnia liberam et quietam in perpetuum per antiquum diuisam scilicet a cilio montis contra
horuordresdene usque in uallem sicut riuulus decurrit in thwedam . ita tamen ut uia
pateat libera pergentibus de horuerdresdene ad eandem Thwedam .

His testibus .
Ernaldo abbate de Chelchehou . Ricardo . Iohanne . Rainaldo . monachis eius . Rabel del
chene . Toma abbet . Iohanne de baiols . Ricardo de Aldre Vlkil de Haudene . Willelmo
filio thor . Alano filio . Gospatrici .
To all faithful men William de Vieuxpont sends greeting.

Know that, guided by repentence, I return to God, and Saint Cuthbert and his monks of Coldingham, the land about which there had been a dispute between me and the same monks in the time of King David; [land] which I had unlawfully appropriated from them; free and quit from every claim for ever; by the time-honoured boundary, that is, from the brow of the hill opposite Horndean, up to the valley as the burn runs down into the Tweed; so that there should be a free road open for travelling from Horndean to the same Tweed.

With these as witnesses: Arnold the abbot of Kelso; Richard, John, Ranald, his monks; Rabel de Cheyne; Thomas Abbet; John de Baiols; Richard de Aldre; Ulfkil of Hadden; William son of Thor; Alan son of Cospatric.
